title: Lantana microphylla
intro: Lantana microphylla, also known as small buttonweed, is a shrub-like plant native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Lantana microphylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine and as a component in some modern herbal remedies.
taxonomy: Lantana microphylla is classified within the family Verbenaceae, which comprises flowering plants commonly known as verbena or lantana. This species belongs to the genus Lantana, characterized by its clusters of small flowers and shrub-like growth habit. The classification of Lantana microphylla is further defined by its binomial nomenclature, which is a two-part name consisting of the genus name and the species name. The taxonomic classification of Lantana microphylla is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other species within the Lantana genus. The Verbenaceae family is a part of the larger clade Asterids, which includes a diverse range of flowering plants.
uses: Lantana microphylla has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments. Its leaves and flowers are said to possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is reportedly used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. Additionally, Lantana microphylla is used in some cultures to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Lantana microphylla for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Lantana microphylla has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and prevent infections. properties: Lantana microphylla is a plant that has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. These compounds may contribute to the plant's potential medicinal properties and uses. parts: Lantana microphylla is a plant species known for its medicinal properties. The leaves of Lantana microphylla are used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. preparations: Lantana microphylla, also known as the small-flowered lantana, is a plant species that has been used in traditional herbal medicine for various purposes. side_effects: Lantana microphylla, also known as smallflower lantana, is native to Central and South America. However, like other Lantana species, it can be toxic to humans and pets if ingested. If ingested, it can cause symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Skin contact with the plant can also cause irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. In rare cases, consuming large amounts of Lantana microphylla can lead to more severe symptoms, including seizures and respiratory distress.
